title: Feature Metadata
description: JSON Schema for features' `metadata.yaml` files. This is a superset of the devcontainer-feature.json schema at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devcontainers/spec/main/schemas/devContainerFeature.schema.json`, extended with internal-use-only properties (prefixed with an underscore) for richer metadata and documentation generation. All 'description' fields support simple (links, formatting) Markdown and HTML.

version.description: The version of the Feature. Follows the semantic versioning (semver) specification.

keywords.description: List of strings relevant to a user that would search for this Feature.

options.description: Possible user-configurable options for this Feature. The selected options will be passed as environment variables when installing the Feature into the container. The names `prefix`, `prefix_discovery`, `prefix_symlinks`, `prefix_exports`, `prefix_activations`, and `runtime_path` — and any name matching `*_prefix`, `*_prefix_discovery`, `*_prefix_symlinks`, `*_prefix_exports`, or `*_activations` — are permanently reserved for code generation via `_options.prefix` and must never be defined manually here.

propertyNames.description: Option keys must start with an ASCII letter and then contain only ASCII letters, digits, underscores, or hyphens.

method.description: Installation method option. Enum values must be drawn from the known set of installation method identifiers.

_options.description: Internal-use-only metadata for auto-generated options. The generator uses this metadata to dynamically generate the corresponding options in the `options` field and the associated installation script code.

user_file_fetch.description: Internal capability flag: feature accepts user-provided file/URI inputs and should receive shared fetch options.

installer_dir.description: When true, exposes installer_dir as a shared option and emits an INSTALLER_DIR auto-init snippet in the generated header.

gh_repo.description: The GitHub repository (or repositories) that this Feature installs. A plain `owner/repo` string for single-repo features; a named map for features that use multiple repositories, where each entry has a `tool_name` (display name) and a `slug` (`owner/repo`).

oneOf.description: Named map of GitHub repositories used by this Feature.

tool_name.description: Human-friendly name of the tool hosted in this repository.

version.description: Internal contract data for the auto-generated `options.version` option and version-related orchestration. When present, the generator injects a `version` option and `_FEAT_CONTRACT_VERSION_*` bash variables into the install script header.

resolution.description: Version resolution strategy used by the auto-impl of `_feat_resolve_version`.

default.description: Default value for the generated `options.version` option.

description.description: Custom description for the generated `options.version` user option. When set, overrides the resolution-based auto-generated description. Use for `none`-resolution features where the accepted version formats are unique to the tool.

inputs.description: For `resolution: none` features only. Each entry auto-generates the corresponding standard proposal(s) in the `version` user option. Valid values: `stable` (→ `stable` proposal), `latest` (→ `latest` proposal), `semver` (→ `1`, `1.2`, `1.2.3`, `1.2.3-rc1`, `1.2.3+build.1` examples), `nvm_alias` (→ `lts/*` and `lts`), `npm_tag` (reserved; `next` is auto-generated for all `npm`-resolution features).

proposals.description: Feature-specific `{value, description}` proposals appended after auto-generated ones. Must not duplicate values already produced by auto-generation for the feature's resolution type. Use for values in formats not covered by auto-generation (e.g. year-based `2026` for TeX Live, tool-specific aliases).

tag_prefix.description: Filter GitHub releases/tags to those whose tag_name starts with this prefix. Required when the repo hosts multiple release lines under different tag namespaces (e.g. "lib/" to match lib/1.2.3 releases from a shared repo). Affects all version specs including latest and stable, which fall back to paginated listing when this field is set. The full tag (prefix included) is used as {feat.tag} in asset URIs.

flag.description: CLI flag for installed-version query, used by the auto-impl of `_feat_installed_version`. Defaults to `--version`.

uri.description: URI of the sidecar file used for version resolution when `resolution=sidecar`. The file is downloaded and scanned for filenames matching `pattern`.

pattern.description: Filename pattern with `[version]` placeholder for extracting version strings from the sidecar file when `resolution=sidecar` (e.g. `zsh-[version].tar.xz`). The `[version]` marker is parsed by `ver__resolve_from_sidecar` to split filenames into prefix/suffix — it is not a context substitution token and is never expanded by `ctx__expand_pattern`.

method.description: Internal contract data for the auto-generated `options.method` option and install orchestration. When present, the generator injects a `method` option and `_FEAT_CONTRACT_*` bash variables into the install script header.

binary.description: Auto-impl data for `METHOD=binary` installation from a release asset. Enables auto-implementation of `_feat_install_binary` using `install__release_asset`.

asset_uri.description: Full asset URI supporting qualified context tokens (`{feat.version}`, `{feat.tag}`, `{plat.kernel:lower}`, `{plat.machine_release}`, `{os.id}`, `{plat.rust_triple}`, etc.) expanded by `ctx__expand_pattern` at install time. When omitted, the feature must set `BINARY_ASSET_URI` dynamically (e.g. via `__install_run_binary_pre`).

sidecar_uri.description: Full sidecar checksum URI. Supports the same runtime substitutions as `asset_uri`. When omitted, sidecar auto-probe is performed.

binary_src.description: Source binary path(s) within the extracted release archive (suffix-match passed to `--binary-src`). Plain strings install unconditionally. Object entries with optional `when` serialize to `path<TAB>when-atoms` in the `binary_src` option default. Multiple entries install real copies into `${PREFIX}/bin/` via fan-out. Distinct from `companion_bins`, which creates symlinks to the primary binary only.

companion_bins.description: Additional entry-point names to create as symlinks to the primary installed binary in `${PREFIX}/bin/` after install. For multicall binaries that expose multiple commands via symlink (e.g. `pandoc-server → pandoc`).

gpg_key_uri.description: URI of the GPG public key used to verify the binary release asset signature. Generates the `binary_gpg_key_uri` user option.

gpg_sig_uri.description: URI of the detached GPG signature for the binary release asset. Supports the same runtime substitutions as `asset_uri`. Generates the `binary_gpg_sig_uri` user option.

when.description: Runtime conditions required for the `binary` method to be considered feasible by `__resolve_auto_method__`. Evaluated by `ctx__match_when` with qualified when YAML (including `feat.version` operator constraints). If omitted, `BINARY_ASSET_URI` is checked for `{plat.rust_triple}` at runtime as a fallback arch constraint.

package.description: Auto-impl marker for `METHOD=package` installation via the OS package manager. Packages are installed from the generated option `ospkg_manifest_method_package_run` (sourced from `_dependencies.run.method-package` by `__dep_install_for_method__`). `__install_run_package__` provides hook points only.

manifest.description: Legacy unused key. Declare package lists in `_dependencies.run.method-package`; they are emitted as the `ospkg_manifest_method_package_run` option.

registers_as.description: OS package name used by `__resolve_auto_method__` when checking whether the PM can satisfy a pinned `version` spec (`ospkg__has_available_version`). Also used by non-PM install methods to register a dummy Debian package via `ospkg__register_dummy` so downstream `Depends:` constraints are satisfied. When omitted, the primary binary name from `_options.prefix.bins` is used for PM checks (works when package and binary names match). Dummy registration is Debian/Ubuntu (apt) only; no-op on other platforms. The registered version equals the resolved `VERSION` at install time.

when.description: Runtime OS context conditions required for the `package` method to be considered feasible by `__resolve_auto_method__`. Same semantics as `binary.when`. Use `plat.pm` to restrict to specific package manager families (e.g. `plat.pm: [apt, dnf]`); omit to allow all PM families.

upstream-package.description: Auto-impl marker for `METHOD=upstream-package` installation from the tool vendor's own OS package repository. Vendor repo/key setup and package install use the generated option `ospkg_manifest_method_upstream_package_run` (from `_dependencies.run.method-upstream-package`). Enables `__install_run_upstream_package__`, `__update_run_upstream_package__`, and `__uninstall_run_upstream_package__` hook points.

registers_as.description: OS package name used by `__resolve_auto_method__` when checking whether the PM can satisfy a pinned `version` spec, and by non-PM install methods to register a dummy Debian package via `ospkg__register_dummy`. When omitted, the primary binary name from `_options.prefix.bins` is used for PM checks. Dummy registration is Debian/Ubuntu (apt) only; no-op on other platforms. The registered version equals the resolved `VERSION` at install time.

when.description: Runtime OS context conditions required for the `upstream-package` method to be considered feasible by `__resolve_auto_method__`. Same semantics as `binary.when`. Use `plat.pm` to restrict to specific package manager families; use `os.id` to restrict to specific distros (e.g. `os.id: ubuntu` for Ubuntu-only vendor repos).

script.description: Auto-impl data for `METHOD=script` installation via an upstream installer script.

asset_uri.description: Full URI of the installer script to fetch. Supports the same qualified context tokens as `binary.asset_uri` (expanded by `ctx__expand_pattern`).

sidecar_uri.description: Full sidecar checksum URI for the installer script. Supports the same runtime substitutions as `asset_uri`. When omitted, sidecar auto-probe is performed.

args.description: Static arguments to pass to the installer script (used when `_feat_run_installer_script` is not defined). For dynamic args, set `_FEAT_INSTALL_SCRIPT_ARGS` in a `__install_run_script_pre` hook.

cargo.description: Auto-impl data for `METHOD=cargo` installation via `cargo install`. Enables auto-implementation of `_feat_install_cargo`.

args.description: Default arguments passed to `cargo install` when `method=cargo`. One entry per element; becomes the default for the auto-generated `cargo_install_args` option.

npm.description: Auto-impl data for `METHOD=npm` installation via `npm install -g`. Enables auto-implementation of `__install_run_npm__`.

package.description: npm package name to install globally.

args.description: Default arguments passed to `npm install` when `method=npm`. One entry per element; becomes the default for the auto-generated `npm_install_args` option.

npm-bundled.description: Auto-impl data for `METHOD=npm-bundled` installation using a self-contained bundled Node.js runtime. Enables `__install_run_npm_bundled__`, detection via `npm__is_bundled`, and uninstall via `npm__uninstall_bundled`.

package.description: npm package name to install with a bundled Node.js runtime (e.g. `@devcontainers/cli`).

cmd.description: Binary name created by the package (e.g. `devcontainer`). Passed as `--cmd` to `npm__install_bundled`. Defaults to the last path segment of `package` with any leading `@` stripped — must be set explicitly when the binary name differs from that default (e.g. `@devcontainers/cli` → binary is `devcontainer`, not `cli`).

node_version.description: Default Node.js major version for the bundled runtime (e.g. `20`, `22`) or `lts`. Becomes the default for the auto-generated `node_version` option. Defaults to `lts`.

source.description: Marker indicating `METHOD=source` is a valid method for this feature. The framework auto-impl handles download and extraction; declare `build_system` for declarative builds, optionally `build_env` for exported build-time environment variables, and optionally `install_bins` when the build produces binaries but upstream exposes no suitable install target.

asset_uri.description: URI pattern for the source tarball. Supports the same qualified context tokens as `binary.asset_uri`. Sets the default for the auto-generated `source_asset_uri` option.

sidecar_uri.description: URI of the checksum or signature sidecar file for the source tarball. Sets the default for the auto-generated `source_sidecar_uri` option.

build_system.description: Build system for the auto-impl of `__install_run_source_auto_build__`. `autotools` = `./configure` then `make`. `make` = `make` only (no `./configure`). Omit when providing a `__install_run_source_build` hook.

configure_args.description: Arguments passed to `./configure` when `build_system=autotools`. `--prefix=<PREFIX>` is always appended automatically. Sets the default for the auto-generated `source_configure_args` option.

build_env.description: Environment variable assignments exported for the framework auto-build commands (e.g. `["GOTOOLCHAIN=auto"]`). Plain strings export unconditionally. Object entries with optional `when` serialize to `value<TAB>when-atoms` in the `source_build_env` option default.

make_flags.description: Make variable assignments included in every `make` invocation (e.g. `["USE_LIBPCRE2=YesPlease"]`). Sets the default for the auto-generated `source_make_flags` option.

make_targets.description: Make targets invoked in sequence. When empty the build-system default is used (`autotools` and `make` both default to `["all", "install"]`). Sets the default for the auto-generated `source_make_targets` option.

install_bins.description: Built binary path(s) inside the extracted source tree to copy into `${PREFIX}/bin/` after the framework auto-build succeeds. Plain strings install unconditionally. Object entries with optional `when` serialize to `path<TAB>when-atoms` in the `source_install_bins` option default. Use this for projects that build binaries but do not provide a suitable `make install` target.

fallback_asset_uri.description: Fallback URI for the source tarball when the primary `asset_uri` fetch fails. Supports the same qualified context tokens as `binary.asset_uri`. Sidecar verification is skipped for the fallback. Sets the default for the auto-generated `source_fallback_asset_uri` option.

git-clone.description: Auto-impl data for `METHOD=git-clone`. Clones `GIT_CLONE_URI` to `PREFIX`. `VERSION` (via `version.resolution: git_ref`) is the branch, tag, or SHA. Set `_options.prefix.root/nonroot` for the clone destination. Use `config` to set arbitrary git config key-value pairs after each clone or update.

uri.description: Repository URI to clone. Supports `{feat.version}` and other qualified context tokens expanded by `ctx__expand_pattern`. Sets the default for the auto-generated `git_clone_uri` option.

config.description: Git config key-value pairs to set after each clone and update. Values support `{feat.version}` substitution. Sets the default for the auto-generated `git_clone_config` array option.

completions.description: Internal contract data for the auto-generated `options.shell_completions`, `options.shell_completions_cmd`, and `options.shell_completions_files` options and shell completion installation. When present, the generator emits these options and the template auto-implements `__install_shell_completions__` for bash, zsh, fish, nushell, and elvish.

subcmd.description: CLI subcommand (with any static flags) that generates completion output for a given shell when appended with the shell name. Example: `"completion --shell"` produces `<bin> completion --shell bash`. Sets the default for the auto-generated `shell_completions_cmd` option. Mutually exclusive with `source_files`.

shells.description: Default shells to install completions for. Becomes the default value of the auto-generated `shell_completions` option.

source_files.description: Map of shell name to completion file path relative to PREFIX. Used when completions are pre-built files installed under PREFIX (e.g. from a source build) rather than generated by the binary at install time. Sets the default for the auto-generated `shell_completions_files` option. Mutually exclusive with `subcmd`.

prefix.description: Declares the installable-path machinery for this feature: prefix resolution, symlinks, PATH-export, shell activation, and write-group permissions. When present, the generator emits the `prefix`, `prefix_discovery`, `prefix_symlinks`, `prefix_exports`, `prefix_activations`, `prefix_bins`, `prefix_bin_dir`, `prefix_symlink_root`, `prefix_symlink_nonroot`, `write_group`, `write_users`, and `install_user` user-facing options, and wires the static `resolve_prefix()` and `_prefix_post_install__generated()` helpers in `install.bash`.

root.description: Default prefix path when running as root (uid=0).

nonroot.description: Default prefix path when running as non-root.

bins.description: Binary filenames to symlink and/or export (e.g. `[gh]`, `[conda, mamba]`). Each binary is resolved at `${PREFIX}/{bin_dir}/{name}`. Required unless both `symlink.skip` and `exports.skip` are true.

bin_dir.description: Subdirectory of PREFIX where the target binaries live. Determines the symlink source path (`${PREFIX}/{bin_dir}/{name}`) and the directory added to PATH (`${PREFIX}/{bin_dir}`).

platform_overrides.description: Platform-specific prefix defaults evaluated before the uid-based root/nonroot fallback. Entries are tested in order; the first matching `when` condition wins.

items.description: A single platform override entry. Specify `when` plus at least one of `default`, `root`, or `nonroot`.

when.description: WhenSpec evaluated at prefix resolution time. Qualified keys (`os.*`, `plat.*`, `feat.*`) only; operator objects on `feat.version` and other version-like fields use semver ordering.

default.description: Prefix to use when the condition matches, for both root and non-root.

root.description: Prefix to use when the condition matches and running as root (uid=0).

nonroot.description: Prefix to use when the condition matches and running as non-root.

symlink.description: Symlink creation configuration. When absent, symlinks are enabled with default paths. Set `skip: true` to disable the `prefix_symlinks` option entirely.

root.description: Bin directory where symlinks are created when running as root.

nonroot.description: Bin directory where symlinks are created when running as non-root.

skip.description: If true, omit the `prefix_symlinks`, `prefix_symlink_root`, and `prefix_symlink_nonroot` options.

exports.description: PATH-export configuration. When absent, exports are enabled. Set `skip: true` to disable the `prefix_exports` option entirely.

description.description: Text appended to the default user-facing description for the `prefix_exports` option.

activation.description: Shell activation snippet configuration. When present, the `prefix_activations` option is generated and `prefix_activation_snippet()` is called during post-install.

shells.description: Default shell names for the `prefix_activations` option (e.g. `[bash, zsh]`).

description.description: Text appended to the default user-facing description for the `prefix_activations` option.

discovery_snippet.description: Per-shell snippets written to startup files for PATH discovery (via `prefix_discovery: shell`). Use `{feat.prefix}` for the resolved installation prefix, `{plat.kernel}` for the kernel name, `{plat.machine_release}` for the CPU architecture. When a shell is listed here, its snippet overrides the generic PATH-prepend export. Generates `prefix_discovery_snippet_<shell>` user-overridable options.

write_group.description: Write-permission group configuration. When present, the `write_group`, `write_users`, and `install_user` options are generated and `users__set_write_permissions` is called during post-install.

default.description: Default value for the `write_group` option (the OS group name).

applies_when.description: Condition under which the prefix machinery is active at install time. When the condition does not match, `resolve_prefix()` and `_prefix_post_install__generated()` become no-ops.

verify.description: Declares a post-create verification command that confirms the tool was installed correctly. The framework writes `post-create--verification.sh` at install time and emits a `postCreateCommand` entry in `devcontainer-feature.json`.

cmd.description: Binary name to invoke for verification when the feature has no `_options.prefix.bins` (e.g. package-managed tools). Falls back to `_DF_EXPECTED_CMD` when absent.

args.description: Arguments passed to the primary binary (resolved via `_DF_EXPECTED_CMD` or `cmd`) to verify the installation — e.g. `--version` or `info --extended`. Exposed as the user-overridable `install_verification_args` option.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the verification command checks.

keep_repos.description: Additional signal (next to `_options.method.upstream-package`) for incuding the `keep_repos` shared option in the feature. Used by features like `install-os-pkg` that don't have a `_options.method.upstream-package` but still need to support the `keep_repos` behavior.

configure_users.description: When true, auto-injects the four user-resolution options (`add_current_user`, `add_remote_user`, `add_container_user`, `add_users`) and auto-calls `__feat_do_configure_users__` in the install-finish and skip lifecycles. The feature must define `__configure_user <username>` to implement the per-user logic.

_env_vars.description: Canonical path/filename values injected from metadata.shared.yaml. install_script.py emits one bash assignment per key, mapping snake_case `foo_bar` to `_FOO_BAR`.

_internal.description: Free-form internal data for reuse in other metadata fields via pyserials templating (e.g. `get("_internal.foo")`). Not emitted to devcontainer-feature.json.

_files.description: Files to write under `src/<feature-id>/files/` at sync time. Each entry supplies a path relative to the feature's `files/` directory and the file content. Must not collide with git-tracked files in `features/<feature-id>/files/`.

path.description: Output path relative to the feature's `files/` directory (e.g. `entrypoint.sh` or `skel/.zshrc`). Must not be absolute or contain `..` segments.

content.description: File content to write at sync time.

_tool_name.description: The human-friendly name of the underlying tool or project that this Feature installs, used for display purposes in documentation and supporting tools.

platforms.description: List of supported platforms. If omitted, the Feature is assumed to support all platforms. Otherwise, every platform not matching at least one entry in this list is considered unsupported.

items.description: A WhenSpec condition (AND object or OR array of AND objects). The platform is supported when any entry matches.

root.description: Whether the Feature requires root permissions to install. This can be either a boolean value (unconditional) or a list of WhenSpec conditions that determine when root is required.

_dependencies.description: Build and runtime dependency manifests for the feature.

run.description: Runtime OS package dependency manifests. Each group is emitted as a generated `ospkg_manifest_*` option during metadata load (pyserials in `metadata.shared.yaml`) and installed by the template dependency dispatcher.

base.description: Runtime dependencies always installed during `__install_init__` via `ospkg_manifest_base_run`.

^method-(binary|cargo|git-clone|npm|npm-.description: Runtime dependencies for a specific install method declared in `_options.method`; mapped to `ospkg_manifest_method_<method>_run`. The method suffix must match a key under `_options.method` (see root `allOf` conditional rules).

^option-[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$.description: Option-gated runtime dependencies; mapped to `ospkg_manifest_option_<name>` and installed when the matching boolean option is true.

build.description: Install-time OS package dependency manifests. Each group is emitted as a generated `ospkg_manifest_*` option during metadata load (pyserials in `metadata.shared.yaml`) and installed by the template dependency dispatcher.

base.description: Install-time dependencies always installed during `__install_init__` via `ospkg_manifest_base_build`.

^method-(binary|cargo|git-clone|npm|npm-.description: Install-time dependencies for a specific install method declared in `_options.method`; mapped to `ospkg_manifest_method_<method>_build`. The method suffix must match a key under `_options.method` (see root `allOf` conditional rules).

_devcontainer.description: Example devcontainer.json configuration for this Feature, used to generate documentation and devcontainer.json files for live testing and demos.

dependsOn.description: An object of Feature dependencies that must be satisfied before this Feature is installed. Elements follow the same semantics of the features object in devcontainer.json

installsAfter.description: Array of ID's of Features that should execute before this one. Allows control for feature authors on soft dependencies between different Features.

legacyIds.description: Array of old IDs used to publish this Feature. The property is useful for renaming a currently published Feature within a single namespace.

deprecated.description: Indicates that the Feature is deprecated, and will not receive any further updates/support. This property is intended to be used by the supporting tools for highlighting Feature deprecation.

capAdd.description: Passes docker capabilities to include when creating the dev container.

customizations.description: Tool-specific configuration. Each tool should use a JSON object subproperty with a unique name to group its customizations.

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint script that should fire at container start up. When `files/entrypoint.sh` exists the framework copies it to the lifecycle directory automatically.

command.description: The entrypoint command forwarded verbatim as the `entrypoint` string in `devcontainer-feature.json`. When omitted the generator constructs the command automatically as `<entrypoint-path> [args]`.

args.description: Extra arguments appended to the auto-generated entrypoint invocation (e.g. `${containerWorkspaceFolder}/.pixi`). Only used when `command` is omitted.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the entrypoint does.

_conf_vars.description: Names of install-time variables (feature options or framework env vars such as `_REMOTE_USER`) to persist in `entrypoint.sh.conf`. The framework writes `KEY="$VALUE"` for each name; the entrypoint sources the file on startup.

init.description: Adds the tiny init process to the container (--init) when the Feature is used.

mounts.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

privileged.description: Sets privileged mode (--privileged) for the container.

securityOpt.description: Sets container security options to include when creating the container.

onCreateCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container. This command is run after "initializeCommand" and before "updateContentCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

updateContentCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container and rerun when the workspace content was updated while creating the container. This command is run after "onCreateCommand" and before "postCreateCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postCreateCommand.description: A command to run after creating the container. This command is run after "updateContentCommand" and before "postStartCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postStartCommand.description: A command to run after starting the container. This command is run after "postCreateCommand" and before "postAttachCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postAttachCommand.description: A command to run when attaching to the container. This command is run after "postStartCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

documentationURL.description: URL to documentation for the Feature.

licenseURL.description: URL to the license for the Feature.

_oci_ref.description: Internal-use-only OCI reference for this Feature

_lifecycle_key_prefix.description: Internal-use-only prefix for lifecycle command keys. With this prefix, the generator rewrites lifecycle command keys from `install` to `<prefix>--install`, etc. This allows multiple features to declare lifecycle commands without risk of key collisions, since each feature gets its own unique prefix.

id.description: ID of the Feature. The id should be unique in the context of the repository/published package where the feature exists and must match the name of the directory where the devcontainer-feature.json resides.

MethodDependencyRequiresBinary.description: When `method-binary` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.binary` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresCargo.description: When `method-cargo` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.cargo` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresGitClone.description: When `method-git-clone` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.git-clone` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresNpm.description: When `method-npm` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.npm` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresNpmBundled.description: When `method-npm-bundled` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.npm-bundled` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresPackage.description: When `method-package` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.package` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresScript.description: When `method-script` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.script` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresSource.description: When `method-source` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.source` must be declared.

MethodDependencyRequiresUpstreamPackage.description: When `method-upstream-package` appears under `_dependencies.run` or `_dependencies.build`, `_options.method.upstream-package` must be declared.

ContentOrUriOptionField.description: Valid only on string-type options. If true (or an object), the option value may be inline multi-line file content, a URI (http(s)://, gh://, oci://, file://, or local path). The generated install-script header normalises literal \n escapes, materialises inline content to a session-scoped temp file, fetches remote URIs, and passes existing local paths through — rewriting the variable to the resolved local path in every case. Non-existing local paths can optionally be passed through unchanged (allow_nonexistent: true) for workspace-mounted files available only at lifecycle-hook time. Materialization uses a session temp dir (cleaned by the exit trap); features must copy to a persistent location if needed.

allow_nonexistent.description: When true, a local path that does not exist at parse time is passed through unchanged instead of raising an error. Default: false.

JsonschemaOptionField.description: Valid only on string-type options. Validate the (already-resolved) option value as a file path against a JSON Schema using sourcemeta/jsonschema (bootstrapped automatically). Validation runs after all option resolution steps. The schema path is relative to the feature directory (${_FEAT_DIR} is automatically prepended by the generator). When allow_nonexistent is true, validation is silently skipped if the file does not exist.

oneOf.description: Path to the JSON Schema file, relative to the feature directory.

schema.description: Path to the JSON Schema file, relative to the feature directory.

allow_nonexistent.description: When true, skip validation if the file does not exist at parse time. Default: false.

FeatureOption.description: Feature option. Two internal-use-only array types are added in addition to the standard boolean and string types for script generation purposes. Externally, these array types are represented as string options where array values are newline-delimited.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

_default.description: Internal-use-only default value for when the user-facing default must be dynamic (i.e. `default: ''`). The generator will use this value instead of `default` when generating the installation script.

enum.description: Allowed values for this option. Unlike 'proposals', the user cannot provide a custom value not included in the 'enum' array.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

proposals.description: Suggested values for this option. Unlike 'enum', the 'proposals' attribute indicates the installation script can handle arbitrary values provided by the user.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

_applies_when.description: Condition under which this option is shown/active in supporting tools and considered applicable by the generator. When the condition does not match, the option is hidden and its value is ignored.

_path.description: Bash file-test(s) the option value must satisfy. A single operator string or an array of operators; each is checked independently. Standard operators: '-e' (exists), '-f' (regular file), '-d' (directory), '-r' (readable), '-w' (writable), '-x' (executable). Prefix with '! ' to invert. Validation is skipped when the value is empty.

_uri.description: If true (or an object), the option value may be a URI (http(s)://, gh://, oci://, file://, or local path with optional #sha256 fragment). The generated install-script header will resolve it to a local filesystem path before running validations (including _path): under ${INSTALLER_DIR}/uri/<option>/ when installer_dir is enabled, otherwise under a private file__mktmpdir directory. For array options, each element is resolved. When object form is used, additional controls may be specified.

chmod.description: chmod mode applied after materializing fetched content (e.g. "600" for secret files, "+x" for scripts). Not applied when the value is passed through as an existing local path.

chmod_exec.description: Deprecated: use chmod: "+x" instead. When true, resolved file(s) are made executable after fetch.

_minimum.description: Minimum allowed value (inclusive) for options of type 'integer'.

_maximum.description: Maximum allowed value (inclusive) for options of type 'integer'.

Mount.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

type.description: Type of mount. Can be 'bind' or 'volume'.

LifecycleCommand.description: A set of commands to run at a specific lifecycle event. Write short per-task keys (e.g. ``install``, ``hello-world``); tooling expands them to ``<owner>-<repo>--<feature-id>--<task>`` when generating ``devcontainer-feature.json``. Legacy keys that already contain ``--<feature-id>--`` are rewritten to the same canonical form.

additionalProperties.description: A command entry with an executable command and a human-readable description of its purpose.

command.description: If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell. When omitted the generator constructs the command automatically as `<lifecycle-path-prefix><task>.sh [args]`.

args.description: Extra arguments appended to the auto-generated script invocation (e.g. `${containerWorkspaceFolder}`). Only used when `command` is omitted.

description.description: A short description of what this command does.

_conf_vars.description: Names of install-time variables (feature options or framework env vars) to persist in the lifecycle script's `.conf` file. The framework writes `KEY="$VALUE"` for each name; the script sources the file at container-creation time.

MethodValue.description: Known installation method values across DevFeats features. Features define their subset via their 'method' option's 'enum' array.

appliesWhen.description: Internal-use-only predicate indicating when an option or prefix group is applicable, based on the values of other options. Multiple conditions may be provided; they are OR-ed together (any one match is sufficient). Within a single condition object, all key=value pairs are AND-ed together.

items.description: A condition for when this option is applicable. This is an object where keys are option names and values are the required values for those options. All conditions in a single object are AND-ed together.

additionalProperties.description: The value of the other option that makes this option applicable. If an array of values is provided, values are OR-ed together.

WhenSpec.description: Unified runtime/platform condition block (AND object or OR array of AND objects). Canonical definition: install-os-pkg/manifest.schema.json#/$defs/WhenSpec.

ContextKeyRegistry.description: Registry of keys populated in ctx at runtime (base keys only; case flavors such as `:lower` are valid only in `{token}` pattern expansion, not in WhenSpec keys). Used for documentation and schema parity tests; not validated against feature metadata at load time.

namespace.description: Context namespace prefix for qualified when keys and pattern tokens.

key.description: Base key name within the namespace (no flavor suffix).

matchMode.description: How when/runtime matching compares values: case-insensitive string equality, or whitespace-separated token membership (`os.id_like`).
